title: Italian European Parliament Elections 2019: Sovereignism vs Europeanism

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on average of Ipsos/SWG polls for March 2019
Estimated: Trend line interpolation from March to April

events.description: Matteo Salvini intensifies campaign focus on European migration policy and sovereignism.
Milan rally of European nationalist parties hosted by Lega.
Internal government tensions between Lega and M5S regarding infrastructure projects.

agents.name: Matteo Salvini
agents.description: Leader of Lega, primary driver of the pro-Lega sentiment.

agents.name: Luigi Di Maio
agents.description: Leader of M5S, coalition partner often in conflict with Lega.

agents.name: Partito Democratico
agents.description: Main opposition party, strongly against Lega's platform.

agents.name: Northern Industrialists
agents.description: Traditional Lega support base, focused on economic stability.

agents.name: Southern Voters
agents.description: Historically skeptical of Lega, targeted by 2019 expansion efforts.

agents.name: Catholic Church Leadership
agents.description: Often critical of Lega's hardline stance on migration.

notes: Pro-side ridefinito come voto a partiti sovranisti (Lega 34.3% + FdI 6.4% = 40.7%, arrotondato a 40.0%). Contro-side = partiti europeisti (PD + FI + +Europa + Verdi). Scelta modellistica: il voto M5S (17.1%) non è incluso in nessuno dei due poli per ambiguità di posizionamento. Polling trajectory è stimata su base trend Ipsos/SWG.
